sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.105 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: b) Let${ V}$be a variety of groups. Its relatively free group of given rank has a presentation$F/N$, where$F$is absolutely free of the same rank and$N$fully invariant in$F$. The associated Lie ring${ L}(F/N)$has a presentation${ L}/J$, where${ L}$is the free Lie ring of the same rank and$J$an ideal of${ L}$. Is$J$fully invariant in${ L}$if${ V}$is the Burnside variety of all groups of given expone...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
